api/Utils.Arrays.ArrayUtils.html.title: Class ArrayUtils | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Arrays.ArrayUtils.html.summary: Class ArrayUtils Namespace Utils.Arrays Assembly Utils.dll Provides helper methods for manipulating arrays, including slicing and trimming utilities. public static class ArrayUtils Inheritance object ArrayUtils Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Adjust<T>(T[], bool, int) Adjusts the size of the array to fullLength, optionally reversing the order of new elements if invert is true. public static T[] Adjust<T>(this T[] array, bool invert, int fullLength) Parameters array T[] The source array. invert bool If true, the existing elements remain in the same order at the start of the new array. If false, they are placed starting from the end of the new array. fullLength int The final desired length. Returns T[] A new array of length fullLength with the original elements in the specified arrangement. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if fullLength is negative. ConvertToArrayOf(IEnumerable<string>, Type) Converts a collection of strings values into an array of a specified elementType. public static Array ConvertToArrayOf(this IEnumerable<string> values, Type elementType) Parameters values IEnumerable<string> The source collection of strings. elementType Type The target element type. Returns Array A new array of type elementType containing parsed values from values. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if values or elementType is null. FormatException May be thrown if parsing fails for a given string. ConvertToArrayOf<T>(IEnumerable<string>) Converts a collection of strings values into an array of type T. public static T[] ConvertToArrayOf<T>(this IEnumerable<string> values) Parameters values IEnumerable<string> The source collection of strings. Returns T[] A new array of type T containing parsed values. Type Parameters T The target element type. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if values is null. FormatException May be thrown if parsing fails for a given string. Copy(Array) Creates a copy of a multidimensional array (2D/3D/etc.). public static Array Copy(Array array) Parameters array Array The source multidimensional array. Returns Array A new multidimensional array with the same dimensions and contents. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. Copy<T>(T[], int) Copies the array array starting at start until the end into a new array. public static T[] Copy<T>(this T[] array, int start) Parameters array T[] The source array. start int The starting index. Returns T[] A new array containing elements from start to the end. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if start is invalid. Copy<T>(T[], int, int) Copies a portion of the array array (from start over length elements) into a new array. public static T[] Copy<T>(this T[] array, int start, int length) Parameters array T[] The source array. start int The starting index of the slice. length int The number of elements to copy. Returns T[] A new array containing the copied elements. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if start or length is invalid. EndWith<T>(T[], params T[]) Checks whether the array array ends with the sequence suffix. public static bool EndWith<T>(this T[] array, params T[] suffix) Parameters array T[] The source array. suffix T[] The sequence to check at the end. Returns bool True if array ends with suffix, false otherwise. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array or suffix is null. Mid<T>(T[], int) Retrieves a slice (sub-part) of this array starting at the specified start index to the end. public static T[] Mid<T>(this T[] s, int start) Parameters s T[] The source array from which to extract the slice. start int The starting index. Can be negative, in which case it counts from the end of the array. For example, -1 would refer to the last element. Returns T[] A new array containing elements from start to the end. Returns an empty array if start is out of range. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if s is null. Mid<T>(T[], int, int) Retrieves a slice (sub-part) of this array. The slice starts at the specified start index and contains length elements. public static T[] Mid<T>(this T[] s, int start, int length) Parameters s T[] The source array from which to extract the slice. start int The starting index of the slice. Can be negative, in which case it counts from the end of the array. For example, -1 would refer to the last element. length int The number of elements to extract. Returns T[] A new array containing the requested slice. If start is out of range, this may return Empty<T>(). Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if s is null. PadLeft<T>(T[], int, T) Resizes the array to have length length, padding on the left with value if necessary. public static T[] PadLeft<T>(this T[] array, int length, T value = default) Parameters array T[] The source array. length int The new length of the array. value T The padding value to insert on the left if the array is enlarged. Returns T[] A new array of size length, padded on the left as needed. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if length is smaller than the current array size. PadRight<T>(T[], int, T) Resizes the array to have length length, padding on the right with value if necessary. public static T[] PadRight<T>(this T[] array, int length, T value = default) Parameters array T[] The source array. length int The new length of the array. value T The padding value to insert on the right if the array is enlarged. Returns T[] A new array of size length, padded on the right as needed. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if length is smaller than the current array size. Replace<T>(T[], T[], T[]) Replaces non-overlapping occurrences of the subsequence toReplace in array with the subsequence replacement. public static T[] Replace<T>(this T[] array, T[] toReplace, T[] replacement) where T : IEquatable<T> Parameters array T[] The source array in which to perform replacements. toReplace T[] The subsequence to find and replace. replacement T[] The subsequence to substitute in place of toReplace. Returns T[] A new array with the replacements applied where the pattern is matched. Type Parameters T Type of elements, must implement IEquatable<T> for equality checks. Remarks Overlapping matches are not detected. Only distinct, non-overlapping occurrences are replaced. If toReplace is empty, no replacement is done and the original array is returned as-is. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array, toReplace, or replacement is null. StartWith(ReadOnlySpan<char>, ReadOnlySpan<char>) Determines whether the provided span begins with the specified prefix. public static bool StartWith(this ReadOnlySpan<char> span, ReadOnlySpan<char> prefix) Parameters span ReadOnlySpan<char> The span to inspect. prefix ReadOnlySpan<char> The expected prefix. Returns bool true when the span starts with prefix. StartWith<T>(T[], params T[]) Checks whether the array array starts with the sequence prefix. public static bool StartWith<T>(this T[] array, params T[] prefix) Parameters array T[] The source array. prefix T[] The sequence to check at the start. Returns bool True if array starts with prefix, false otherwise. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array or prefix is null. TrimEnd<T>(T[], Func<T, bool>) Returns a new array without the elements at the end that match the given trimTester predicate. public static T[] TrimEnd<T>(this T[] obj, Func<T, bool> trimTester) Parameters obj T[] The source array. trimTester Func<T, bool> A function that returns true for elements that should be trimmed from the end. Returns T[] A new array with matching elements removed from the end only. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if obj or trimTester is null. TrimEnd<T>(T[], params T[]) Returns a new array without the values at the end (those in values). public static T[] TrimEnd<T>(this T[] obj, params T[] values) Parameters obj T[] The source array. values T[] Values to remove from the end. Returns T[] A new array with those values removed from the end only. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if obj or values is null. TrimStart<T>(T[], Func<T, bool>) Returns a new array without the elements at the start that match the given trimTester predicate. public static T[] TrimStart<T>(this T[] obj, Func<T, bool> trimTester) Parameters obj T[] The source array. trimTester Func<T, bool> A function that returns true for elements that should be trimmed from the start. Returns T[] A new array with matching elements removed from the start only. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if obj or trimTester is null. InnerJoin = 0 Returns only elements that appear in both sequences. LeftJoin = 1 Includes all elements from the left sequence, pairing them with matching right elements when available. RightJoin = 2 Includes all elements from the right sequence, pairing them with matching left elements when available.

If the element is inserted before Utils.Collections.SkipList<T>._firstElement, it becomes the new first. If it's inserted after Utils.Collections.SkipList<T>._lastElement, it becomes the new last. api/Utils.Collections.html.title: Namespace Utils.Collections | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Collections.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Collections Classes CachedLoader<TKey, TValue> A class that provides a caching mechanism for loading resources. If a resource is not already loaded, it will be loaded and cached upon first access. DictionaryExtensions Provides thread-safe helper methods for working with Dictionary<TKey, TValue> instances. DictionaryMap<K, V> Implementation of IDictionaryMap<K, V> that wraps custom handlers for dictionary operations. DoubleIndexedDictionary<T1, T2> Represents a dictionary that maintains two-way mappings between keys of type T1 and values of type T2. This allows bidirectional access by both key and value. EnumerableComparer<T> Compares two sequences of values of comparable types. Supports custom comparers and optimizations for IReadOnlyList<T> and Span<T> where applicable. EnumerableEqualityComparer<T> A comparer for enumerables that allows comparison of sequences based on custom or default equality logic for each element. If both sequences implement IReadOnlyList<T>, the comparison first checks Count for an early exit. Uses Span<T> when applicable for performance. EnumerableEx Extension methods for working with IEnumerable<T> and related collections. ForwardFusion<T1, T2> Merges two sorted IEnumerable<T> collections based on the specified join type. The left list is considered the primary list in a left join, and vice versa for a right join. IndexedList<K, V> Indexed list. LRUCache<K, V> A Least Recently Used (LRU) cache implementation. This cache evicts the least recently accessed items when it reaches its capacity. MappedDictionary<K, V> A dictionary that uses a custom mapping for its operations. It allows customization of how the dictionary handles additions, removals, and value retrievals. ReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> Default implementation of IReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> that relies on delegate accessors. ReadOnlyMappedDictionary<K, V> List whose source is defined by accessors. SkipList<T> Represents a skip list, which is a probabilistic data structure that allows for fast search, insertion, and deletion operations. SymbolLeaf Represents a node (leaf) within the SymbolTree. Stores one character and potentially references child nodes. SymbolTree Represents a prefix tree (trie) structure for storing and retrieving symbols (strings) efficiently. Structs Pack<T> Represents a value and its consecutive repetition count (used in packing). Interfaces IDictionaryMap<K, V> Interface representing a modifiable map that supports dictionary operations. IReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> Defines accessor methods required to expose dictionary data without granting mutation capabilities. Enums JoinType Specifies the type of join operation. Delegates CachedLoader<TKey, TValue>.TryLoadValueDelegate Represents a method that attempts to load a value associated with the specified key.

api/Utils.Dates.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Dates Classes DateFormula Evaluates date formulas using culture specific tokens. DateFormulaLanguage Represents culture specific tokens used to interpret date formulas. DateUtils Provides utility methods for date and time calculations and manipulations. JsonDateFormulaLanguageProvider Loads DateFormulaLanguage data from a JSON configuration. WeekUtils Provides helper methods to calculate week numbers and their associated date ranges for a given year. Structs Week Represents a specific week in a given year. Interfaces ICalendarProvider Provides access to calendar-specific information about working and non-working days. IDateFormulaLanguageProvider Provides culture specific DateFormulaLanguage instances. Enums PeriodTypeEnum Defines different types of time periods.

api/Utils.DependencyInjection.html.title: Namespace Utils.DependencyInjection | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.DependencyInjection.html.summary: Namespace Utils.DependencyInjection Classes HandlerCaller Default implementation of IHandlerCaller using IServiceProvider. InjectableAttribute Marks an interface as available for dependency injection. Implementations of interfaces decorated with this attribute can be automatically registered in the service container. InjectableClassAttribute Base attribute for injectable classes specifying the registration domain. ScopedAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered with a scoped lifetime. ServiceConfigurationHelper Helper methods to configure services based on injectable attributes. SingletonAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered as a singleton service. StaticAutoAttribute Applies to an IServiceConfigurator implementation to trigger compile-time generation of service registrations. TransientAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered with a transient lifetime. Structs CheckError<E> Represents a validation error produced by a specific check. Interfaces ICheck<T, E> Validates messages before they are handled. IHandlerCaller Dispatches messages to their associated handlers. IHandler<T> Defines a message handler for a specific message type. IInjectable Marker interface for services that can be injected. IServiceConfigurator Configures dependency injection services for an application. Implementations can customize registrations for a given IServiceCollection.

Circle(PointF, float, float, double, double, double) Initializes a new instance of the Circle class representing an ellipse arc. public Circle(PointF center, float radius1, float radius2, double orientation = 0, double startAngle = 0, double endAngle = 6.283185307179586) Parameters center PointF Center of the ellipse. radius1 float Radius along the first axis. radius2 float Radius along the second axis. orientation double Rotation of the ellipse in radians. startAngle double Starting angle of the arc in radians. endAngle double Ending angle of the arc in radians. Properties Center Gets the geometric center of the circle or ellipse. public PointF Center { get; } Property Value PointF EndAngle Gets the angle, in radians, at which the arc ends. public double EndAngle { get; } Property Value double Length Gets the perimeter length of the computed polyline representation. public float Length { get; } Property Value float Orientation Gets the rotation of the ellipse around its center in radians. public double Orientation { get; } Property Value double Radius1 Gets the first radius of the ellipse. When equal to Radius2 it represents the radius of a circle. public float Radius1 { get; } Property Value float Radius2 Gets the second radius of the ellipse. public float Radius2 { get; } Property Value float StartAngle Gets the angle, in radians, at which the arc starts. public double StartAngle { get; } Property Value double Methods GetPoints(bool, float) Returns oriented points along the ellipse arc. public IEnumerable<DrawPoint> GetPoints(bool closed, float position = 0) Parameters closed bool Indicates whether the sequence should be closed. position float Starting accumulated length value for the first point. GetSegments(bool) Returns segments describing the ellipse arc. public IEnumerable<Segment> GetSegments(bool closed) Parameters closed bool Indicates whether the returned segments should form a closed path. Returns IEnumerable<Segment> Enumeration of segments representing the ellipse.

Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Remarks The floating-point API mirrors the integer-based DrawI<T> helpers while transparently converting coordinates expressed in an arbitrary viewport to pixel positions. This allows callers to operate in normalized or physical spaces without manually performing the conversions required by the underlying IImageAccessor<T> implementation. All conversions clamp to the image bounds when delegated to Draw, ensuring consistency with the low-level routines. Constructors DrawF(IImageAccessor<T>) Initializes a new instance of the DrawF<T> class covering the entire image. public DrawF(IImageAccessor<T> imageAccessor) Parameters imageAccessor IImageAccessor<T> Accessor used to manipulate the underlying image. Remarks The viewport matches the full image bounds so one drawing unit equals one pixel. DrawF(IImageAccessor<T>, float, float, float, float) Initializes a new instance of the DrawF<T> class using a custom viewport. public DrawF(IImageAccessor<T> imageAccessor, float top, float left, float right, float down) Parameters imageAccessor IImageAccessor<T> Accessor used to manipulate the underlying image. top float Top boundary of the viewport. left float Left boundary of the viewport. right float Right boundary of the viewport. down float Bottom boundary of the viewport. Remarks The viewport boundaries define the coordinate space exposed to callers; the constructor computes the scaling factors that map the viewport to pixel positions. Properties Down Gets the bottom coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Down { get; } Property Value float Draw Gets the integer-based drawing helper that ultimately performs the rasterization work. public DrawI<T> Draw { get; } Property Value DrawI<T> Left Gets the left coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Left { get; } Property Value float Right Gets the right coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Right { get; } Property Value float Top Gets the top coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Top { get; } Property Value float Methods ComputePixelPosition(PointF) Computes the integer pixel corresponding to the provided floating-point coordinates. public Point ComputePixelPosition(PointF p) Parameters p PointF Coordinates expressed in the viewport space. Returns Point The integer pixel location within the image. Remarks The conversion applies the viewport scaling and truncates the result to the nearest lower integer, matching the behavior of Floor(double) for positive coordinates. ComputePixelPosition(float, float) Computes the integer pixel corresponding to the provided floating-point coordinates. public Point ComputePixelPosition(float x, float y) Parameters x float Horizontal coordinate in the viewport space. y float Vertical coordinate in the viewport space. Returns Point The integer pixel location within the image. Remarks Values falling outside the viewport range result in negative or oversized pixel indices; the downstream Draw helper clamps them to the image dimensions when actually drawing. ComputePixelPositionF(PointF) Computes the floating-point pixel location corresponding to the provided viewport coordinates. public PointF ComputePixelPositionF(PointF p) Parameters p PointF Coordinates expressed in the viewport space. Returns PointF The floating-point pixel position inside the image. Remarks The method preserves the fractional part, which is useful for anti-aliasing or sub-pixel sampling scenarios. ComputePixelPositionF(float, float) Computes the floating-point pixel location corresponding to the provided viewport coordinates. public PointF ComputePixelPositionF(float x, float y) Parameters x float Horizontal coordinate in the viewport space. y float Vertical coordinate in the viewport space. Returns PointF The floating-point pixel position inside the image. Remarks Unlike ComputePixelPosition(float, float), this overload returns sub-pixel precision so callers can implement their own interpolation strategies. api/Utils.Drawing.html.title: Namespace Utils.Drawing | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Drawing.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Drawing Classes BaseDrawing<T> Provides a base implementation that gives access to an image accessor for derived drawing helpers. Bezier Represents a Bézier curve that can be rasterized to points or line segments. Circle Represents an ellipse or circle drawable approximation that can be converted into points or segments. DrawF<T> Provides floating-point drawing helpers on top of DrawI<T>. DrawI<T> Provides integer-based drawing helpers for image accessors. DrawPoint Represents a sampled point produced by a drawing algorithm. MapBrush<T> Represents a brush that caches generated points to avoid drawing duplicates. Path Represents a drawable path composed of straight and Bézier segments. Pathes Represents a simple collection of drawable paths. Polygon Represents a closed polygon composed of connected segments. Polylines Represents a sequence of connected segments that are not necessarily closed. Segment Represents a straight line segment and exposes helper information used by raster drawing routines. Interfaces IBrush<T> Defines the contract for drawing brushes. IDrawable Describes an object that can be converted into drawing primitives such as segments or oriented points. Delegates DrawingColorMap<T> Represents a function that maps a drawing position and offset to a color value. UVMap<T> Represents a function that maps two-dimensional UV coordinates to a color value.

api/Utils.Expressions.ExpressionBuilders.title: Namespace Utils.Expressions.ExpressionBuilders | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Expressions.ExpressionBuilders.summary: Namespace Utils.Expressions.ExpressionBuilders Classes AddAssignationBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for an additive assign operation (e.g. "+="), supporting string concatenation when the left operand is a string. AssignationBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for simple assignment operators (e.g. "="), allowing a custom assignment BinaryExpression factory. BlockBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for a block expression, e.g. statements within { ... }. Each expression read inside becomes part of the block, using a BlockExpressionBuilder. BracketBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for bracket-based operations, which may represent either array indexing or default member (indexer) access. BreakBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for the "break" statement, returning an Break(LabelTarget) to the nearest loop label. CloseBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder that checks for matching wrap start and sets the flag, concluding the bracket/parenthesis context. ConditionalBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder and IAdditionalTokens for a ternary-like operation, e.g., "condition ? expr : expr". The symbol recognized for the second separator is customizable via ChoiceSymbol. ContinueBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for the "continue" statement, returning an Break(LabelTarget) that effectively simulates a continue (jumping to the loop's continue label). DefaultUnaryBuilder A default unary builder that determines whether the token is a name (resolving a parameter, constant, or type) or a string/char literal, and creates the appropriate Expression. FalseBuilder Builds an expression representing false. ForBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for a "for" loop construct, building an Loop(Expression) with initializers, condition, and increments. ForEachBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for a "foreach" loop, building either an BlockExpression that enumerates an array or uses IEnumerator for other enumerables. IfBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for "if" statements, optionally handling an "else" branch. MemberBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder to handle a member access operation (e.g., appending ".MemberName" after an existing expression). NewBuilder Builds a new expression for either an object, array, or collection/member initialization. NewBuilder.ReadNextExpressionBuilder Builds an expression by reading the next expression from the parser at the given priority. Useful for sub-scenarios within "new" statements. NewBuilder.UnaryMinusBuilder Implements unary minus builder for negative numeric constants or unary expressions. NullBuilder Builds an expression representing null. NullOrMemberBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for the null-conditional operator (?.), returning either null or the expression member access. NumberConstantBuilder Builds an expression representing a numeric constant. Handles base prefixes (e.g., "0x" for hex), numeric suffixes (e.g., "f" for float), and direct numeric literals, returning the corresponding ConstantExpression. OperatorBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for binary operators, allowing a custom operator function and optional numeric type adjustment. ParenthesisBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for handling parentheses or bracket expressions, such as grouped sub-expressions, type casts, or lambda parameter lists. PlusOperatorBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder to handle the plus operator (+), supporting both numeric addition and string concatenation. PostOperationBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for post-increment or post-decrement operators, e.g. i++ or i--. The supplied operator function is responsible for creating the appropriate unary expression. ReturnBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for the "return" statement. The builder simply reads the following expression and returns it, ignoring early-exit semantics. RightParenthesisBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for a closing parenthesis builder. Depending on the context, this may represent function invocation or fallback logic. SizeOfBuilder Builds an expression for sizeof(T), retrieving the size of the specified type via SizeOf(Type). SwitchBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for switch statements. This builder supports both expression-style switches and the classic statement form where each case ends with break;. ThrowParseException Provides an implementation of both IStartExpressionBuilder and IFollowUpExpressionBuilder that throws a ParseUnknownException whenever an expression is to be built, indicating that the token or symbol cannot be parsed. TrueBuilder Builds an expression representing true. TypeCastBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for type-cast operations such as (Type)expression. It uses Expression.TypeAs to create a safe cast expression. TypeMatchBuilder Implements IFollowUpExpressionBuilder for a type-check operator, e.g. "expression is Type". TypeofBuilder Builds an expression for typeof(T), returning a constant Type object. UnaryOperandBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for unary operators, building a unary expression using the provided OperatorBuilder function. WhileBuilder Implements IStartExpressionBuilder for a "while" loop construct, building an Loop(Expression). The loop terminates if the condition is false.

api/Utils.Expressions.ExpressionSignatur.summary: Class ExpressionSignatureAttribute Namespace Utils.Expressions Assembly Utils.dll Marks a method or parameter as having a signature requirement for a certain ExpressionType. During construction, it gathers all methods marked with ExpressionSignatureAttribute from the derived type. protected ExpressionTransformer() Methods CopyExpression(Expression, params Expression[]) Creates a new expression of the same ExpressionType as e, using the supplied parameters as sub-expressions or arguments. If certain ExpressionType values are not supported by this switch, they are simply returned as-is or an exception is thrown. protected static Expression CopyExpression(Expression e, params Expression[] parameters) Parameters e Expression The original expression to copy. parameters Expression[] The sub-expressions to insert into the copied expression. Returns Expression A new expression replicating the structure of e with possibly different sub-expressions. FinalizeExpression(Expression, Expression[]) Called if no custom transformation method (annotated with ExpressionSignatureAttribute) is found. Allows final post-processing. The default implementation throws an exception. protected virtual Expression FinalizeExpression(Expression e, Expression[] parameters) Parameters e Expression The expression being finalized. parameters Expression[] The sub-expressions or operands for e. Returns Expression A finalized expression. Exceptions Exception Thrown by default to indicate that transformation cannot be completed. PrepareExpression(Expression) Prepares an expression for transformation. Subclasses can override this to apply initial logic before the main Transform(Expression) switch (e.g., caching). The default implementation returns the expression unchanged. protected virtual Expression PrepareExpression(Expression e) Parameters e Expression The expression to prepare. ReplaceArguments(Expression, ParameterExpression[], Expression[]) Replaces all occurrences of oldParameters within e with the corresponding items in newParameters. protected Expression ReplaceArguments(Expression e, ParameterExpression[] oldParameters, Expression[] newParameters) Parameters e Expression The expression in which parameter references are replaced. oldParameters ParameterExpression[] The parameters to remove. newParameters Expression[] The new expressions that replace oldParameters. This method checks for known signatures (via ExpressionSignatureAttribute-annotated methods) and if a match is found, invokes the corresponding transformation function. If no signature method matches, it calls FinalizeExpression(Expression, Expression[]) by default. protected Expression Transform(Expression e) Parameters e Expression The expression to transform. Returns Expression A possibly rewritten expression.

Expression Build(ExpressionParserCore parser, ParserContext context, Expression currentExpression, string val, string nextVal, int priorityLevel, ref int nextLevel, Parenthesis markers, ref bool isClosedWrap) Parameters parser ExpressionParserCore An ExpressionParserCore orchestrating the overall parsing. context ParserContext The current ParserContext state. currentExpression Expression The existing expression subtree built so far. val string The token most recently read. nextVal string The next token to process (e.g., an operator). priorityLevel int The current parsing priority. nextLevel int A reference integer indicating the next operator's priority level. ResolveType(string, Type[]) Attempts to resolve a generic type by its name, using the provided generic parameters. public Type ResolveType(string name, Type[] genericParameters) Parameters name string The name of the type to resolve. genericParameters Type[] An array of types to use as generic arguments. Returns Type A constructed Type object if resolution is successful; otherwise null. SelectConstructor(IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo>, Expression[]) Selects the most suitable constructor from the provided constructors based on the specified arguments. This method uses parameter matching and assigns a "distance" score to find the best match. public (ConstructorInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? SelectConstructor(IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo> constructors, Expression[] arguments) Parameters constructors IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo> A sequence of ConstructorInfo candidates. arguments Expression[] The argument expressions used in the constructor call. Returns (ConstructorInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? A tuple containing the ConstructorInfo and the adjusted Expression array, or null if no suitable constructor is found. SelectMethod(IEnumerable<MethodInfo>, Expression, Type[], Expression[]) Selects the most suitable method from the provided methods, optionally applying genericParameters if it's a generic method definition. Determines a "distance" score to pick the best match, then returns the adjusted expressions. public (MethodInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? SelectMethod(IEnumerable<MethodInfo> methods, Expression obj, Type[] genericParameters, Expression[] arguments) Parameters methods IEnumerable<MethodInfo> A sequence of MethodInfo candidates. obj Expression An Expression representing the target object (for instance methods), or null for static calls. genericParameters Type[] Optional array of types to use if the method is generic. arguments Expression[] The argument expressions for the method call. Returns (MethodInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? A tuple containing the MethodInfo and the adjusted Expression array, or null if no suitable method is found. TryGetConstant(string, out ConstantExpression) Attempts to retrieve a named constant from the internal Constants dictionary. public bool TryGetConstant(string name, out ConstantExpression constantExpression) Parameters name string The name of the constant. constantExpression ConstantExpression When this method returns, contains a ConstantExpression of the constant value if found. Returns bool true if the constant was found; otherwise false.

IBuilder builder) Parameters content string The string content to tokenize. builder IBuilder A builder interface providing symbols, whitespace, and token readers. Properties Content Gets the full content string being tokenized. public string Content { get; } Property Value string DefineString Gets a string result defined during the most recent call to ReadToken(bool), if any transformer modified the token content. public string DefineString { get; } Property Value string Position Gets the current tokenizer position (read-only). public ITokenizerPosition Position { get; } Property Value ITokenizerPosition StringTransformers Gets an enumeration of transformers that can modify string tokens once read. public IEnumerable<StringTransformer> StringTransformers { get; } Property Value IEnumerable<StringTransformer> Symbols Gets the SymbolTree that defines recognized symbols within the content. public SymbolTree Symbols { get; } Property Value SymbolTree TokenReaders Gets an enumeration of functions that attempt to read tokens from content. public IEnumerable<TryReadToken> TokenReaders { get; } Property Value IEnumerable<TryReadToken> WhiteSpaces Gets the collection of characters considered to be whitespace. public char[] WhiteSpaces { get; } Property Value char[] Methods DiscardPosition() Discards the most recently saved position by popping it from the internal stack, but does not restore it to the current position. public void DiscardPosition() PeekToken() Peeks the next token without advancing the tokenizer's position. public string PeekToken() Returns string The next token, or null if no token is available. PopPosition() Pops a previously saved tokenizer position from the internal stack and restores it as the current position. public void PopPosition() PushPosition() Pushes the current tokenizer position onto an internal stack, allowing you to restore this position later via PopPosition(). public void PushPosition() ReadSymbol(string, bool) Attempts to read a specific symbol from the current position. If successful, advances the position past the symbol. public bool ReadSymbol(string symbol, bool throwExceptionIfError = true) Parameters symbol string The symbol string to match. throwExceptionIfError bool If true, throws an exception if the symbol does not match. Otherwise, returns false on a mismatch. Returns bool true if the symbol was matched; otherwise false. ReadToken(bool) Reads the next token from the content. public string ReadToken(bool isIgnoreWhiteSpace = true) Parameters isIgnoreWhiteSpace bool If true, any whitespace characters are skipped prior to reading. Returns string The token string if read successfully; otherwise null if no more tokens can be read. ResetPosition() Resets the tokenizer position to the beginning of the content, and clears any saved positions. public void ResetPosition()

api/Utils.Expressions.html.title: Namespace Utils.Expressions | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Expressions.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Expressions Classes BlockExpressionBuilder A helper class that builds expression blocks while minimizing the number of declared variables. CompileException Represents the base class for compile-time exceptions that occur during expression compilation in the Utils.Expressions namespace. ConstantNumericAttribute A specialized attribute that indicates the expected parameter is a ConstantExpression holding a numeric value, optionally restricted to a specific set of allowed numeric values. ExpressionCallSignatureAttribute Marks a method as matching only method-call expressions that invoke a specific function name on a specific type. This is a specialized ExpressionSignatureAttribute for calls. ExpressionEx Provides helper methods for building and manipulating expression trees. ExpressionParser Provides static methods for parsing and compiling lambda expressions from string code. ExpressionParserCore Provides core parsing functionality for building expression trees from tokenized input. ExpressionSignatureAttribute Marks a method or parameter as having a signature requirement for a certain ExpressionType. When used on a method, the method is considered for transformation if its attribute matches the current node type. When used on a parameter, it further restricts which sub-expressions are permissible. ExpressionTransformer Provides an abstract base class to transform or rewrite LINQ expression trees. Subclasses may override the transformation logic for specific expression signatures. ExpressionUtils Provides utility helpers for analysing and comparing expression trees. FindTypeException Thrown when a type cannot be found during a type resolution process. FormattedPart Represents a formatted part of an interpolated string, containing an expression and optional format/alignment specifications. InterpolatedStringParser Parses an interpolated string into its constituent parts, which can be either literals or formatted expressions. LiteralPart Represents a literal (non-interpolated) part of a string. Parenthesis Describes a set of parenthesis tokens used by the expression parser. ParseDuplicateParameterNameException Thrown when the parser encounters a parameter name that is already in use. ParseException Represents the base class for all parsing exceptions that occur during expression parsing in the Utils.Expressions namespace. ParseNoEndException Thrown when an expected closing symbol is not found in the parsed expression. ParseUnfindTypeException Thrown when a type name cannot be resolved or found by the parser. ParseUnknownException Thrown when an unknown symbol is encountered by the parser. ParseUnmatchException Thrown when a pair of opening and closing symbols do not match in the parsed expression. ParseWrongSymbolException Thrown when the parser encounters a symbol that is incorrect for the current context. ParserContext Maintains contextual information while parsing expressions. ParserOptions Provides configuration settings and default mappings for parsing expressions, including operator priorities, numeric type levels, default type keywords, and numeric suffix mappings. ReturnTypeAttribute A specialized attribute that indicates the matched expression's return type must be assignable to a specified type. Useful for restricting the type of an operand beyond its ExpressionType. Tokenizer Represents a tokenizer that parses and reads tokens from a given string content, based on configurable symbols, whitespace definitions, and token readers. Interfaces IAdditionalTokens Represents an entity that provides additional tokens beyond the standard set, useful for customizing lexical analysis or parser behavior. IBuilder Describes the configuration and components required to parse and build expressions (e.g., token readers, whitespace handling, etc.). IDistanceValue<T> Represents a pairing of a distance measure and a value, typically used for scoring or ranking operations in method or constructor selection. IFollowUpExpressionBuilder Defines a contract for building follow-up portions of an expression, typically when the parser encounters an operator or token after an initial expression. IInterpolatedStringPart Represents a part of an interpolated string, which can be either literal text or a formatted expression. IParserOptions Describes configuration settings that influence how expressions are parsed and interpreted. IResolver Provides functionality to resolve types, constructors, methods, and members at runtime, as well as to manage constants in expressions. IStartExpressionBuilder Defines a contract for building the initial portion of an expression, typically when the parser encounters a token indicating a new expression start. ITokenizerPosition Exposes read-only properties representing the tokenizer's position within the content. ITypeFinder Provides methods for finding types and extension methods at runtime. Delegates StringTransformer Represents a function that attempts to transform or rewrite a token string. If successful, outputs a new token result. TryReadToken Represents a function that attempts to read a token from a given content string at the specified index. If successful, it outputs the token's length.

api/Utils.Files.PathUtils.html.title: Class PathUtils | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Files.PathUtils.html.summary: Class PathUtils Namespace Utils.Files Assembly Utils.dll Provides helpers for enumerating directories and files when the path contains wildcard segments. Each public method expands intermediate directories before delegating the final segment to the requested enumeration API. public static class PathUtils Inheritance object PathUtils Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods EnumerateDirectories(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates only directories (as DirectoryInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for directories. public static IEnumerable<DirectoryInfo> EnumerateDirectories(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<DirectoryInfo> EnumerateDirectories(string) Enumerates only directories for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateDirectories(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateDirectories(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another*\EndDir??"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified directory paths matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories. EnumerateFileSystemEntries(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates both files and directories (as FileSystemInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for either directories or files. public static IEnumerable<FileSystemInfo> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<FileSystemInfo> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string) Enumerates both files and directories for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another**.*"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified paths (files or directories) matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories. EnumerateFiles(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates only files (as FileInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for files. public static IEnumerable<FileInfo> EnumerateFiles(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<FileInfo> EnumerateFiles(string) Enumerates only files for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateFiles(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFiles(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another*\File??.txt"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified file paths matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories.

} Property Value int[] StandardEncoding Gets the Adobe standard encoding table mapping character codes to glyph indexes. public static int[] StandardEncoding { get; } Property Value int[] StdNames Gets the table of standard glyph names defined by Adobe for Type 1 fonts. public static string[] StdNames { get; GetSpacingCorrection(char, char) Returns the horizontal spacing correction between two adjacent characters. api/Utils.Fonts.PostScript.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.PostScript | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.PostScript.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.PostScript Classes CidKeyedFont Very small loader for CID-Keyed Type 1 PostScript fonts. Only a subset of the format is implemented. Refer to Adobe Technical Note 5014 for the full specification. PostScriptFont Very small PostScript font loader based on a custom text representation. The intention is to mimic a subset of the Type 1 syntax without parsing the full PostScript language. Each glyph is declared as shown below: Glyph: A Width: 500 Height: 600 Baseline: 0 Path: M 0 0 L 10 0 ... EndGlyph For a description of the real Type 1 format see Adobe Technical Note 5040. PostScriptGlyph Represents a glyph defined by a sequence of basic PostScript path commands. The commands correspond loosely to the operators described in Adobe's Type 1 specification. Type3Font Minimal loader for PostScript Type 3 fonts. Only a tiny subset of the language is interpreted: setcachedevice for metrics and the basic path commands moveto, lineto, curveto and closepath. See Adobe Technical Note 5015 for the full details. Type42Font Minimal loader for PostScript Type 42 fonts which encapsulate a TrueType font. This implementation extracts the embedded TrueType data from the /sfnts array and delegates glyph retrieval to TrueTypeFont. For the official format see Adobe Technical Note 5012. Structs PostScriptGlyph.PathCommand Represents a single drawing command extracted from the font. The coordinates use the same units as the original charstring. Enums PostScriptGlyph.PathCommandType Enumeration of possible path command types supported by this simplified glyph representation.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable.html.title: Class HeadTable | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable.html.summary: Class HeadTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.htm.summary: Class AcntTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The accent attachment table (tag: 'acnt') provides a space‑efficient method of combining component glyphs into compound glyphs to form accents. Accented glyphs are a very restricted subclass of compound glyphs. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap Classes CMapFormat0 Represents CMap Format 0 for mapping characters to glyph indices using a simple 256-byte table. CMapFormat4 Represents a CMap Format 4 for mapping characters to glyph indices using segmented arrays. CMapFormat4.DeltaMap Represents a segment defined by a delta value (no explicit mapping table). CMapFormat4.Segment Represents a segment in the CMap Format 4 mapping table. CMapFormat4.TableMap Represents a segment with an explicit mapping table. CMapFormatBase Represents the base class for CMap (character to glyph mapping) formats in a TrueType font.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GlyfTable.htm.summary: Class GlyfTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs). api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HheaTable.htm.summary: Class HheaTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'hhea' table contains information needed to layout fonts whose characters are written horizontally, that is, either left-to-right or right-to-left. It begins with the hMetrics array, where each entry consists of the advance width and left side bearing for a glyph. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LocaTable.htm.title: Class LocaTable | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LocaTable.htm.summary: Class LocaTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'loca' table stores the offsets to the locations of the glyphs in the font relative to the beginning of the 'glyf' table. Its purpose is to provide quick access to the data for a particular glyph. For example, in the standard Macintosh glyph ordering, the character A is the 76th glyph in a font. The 'loca' table stores the offset from the start of the 'glyf' table to the position at which the data for each glyph can be found. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Classes AcntTable The accent attachment table (tag: 'acnt') provides a space‑efficient method of combining component glyphs into compound glyphs to form accents. Accented glyphs are a very restricted subclass of compound glyphs. CmapTable Represents the 'cmap' table which provides the character-to-glyph mapping for a TrueType font. The table contains one or more subtables (each identified by a platform ID and platform-specific ID) that define different mapping formats. CmapTable.CmapSubtable Represents a subtable identifier defined by a platform ID and platform-specific ID. Used as a key for the cmap subtables. CvtTable The CVT (Control Value Table) contains a series of signed 16-bit integers that are used as control values during the hinting process. These values are used by the font program to adjust glyph outlines. FpgmTable The FPGM (Font Program) table contains a set of instructions (bytecode) that are executed once when the font is loaded. These instructions establish routines used for hinting glyphs. GlyfTable The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs). HheaTable The 'hhea' table contains information needed to layout fonts whose characters are written horizontally, that is, either left-to-right or right-to-left. This table provides global metrics for the font, including typographic values such as ascent, descent, line gap, and other essential data used during glyph layout. HmtxTable The 'hmtx' table contains metric information for the horizontal layout of each glyph in the font. It begins with the hMetrics array, where each entry consists of the advance width and left side bearing for a glyph. The number of long horizontal metrics is specified in the 'hhea' table, and in a monospaced font, only one entry is required (but must still be present). KernTable Represents the 'kern' table which contains kerning pairs for adjusting the spacing between glyphs in a TrueType font. This implementation supports the most common format (version 0) with a single horizontal kerning subtable. LocaTable The 'loca' table stores the offsets to the locations of the glyphs in the font relative to the beginning of the 'glyf' table. Its purpose is to provide quick access to the data for a particular glyph. For example, in the standard Macintosh glyph ordering, the character A is the 76th glyph in a font. The 'loca' table stores the offset from the start of the 'glyf' table to the position at which the data for each glyph can be found. MaxpTable The 'maxp' table establishes the memory requirements for a font. It begins with a table version number and the number of glyphs in the font. The remaining entries set maximum values for various parameters (such as maximum points, contours, and storage), which are used to allocate resources for the font. PostTable The 'post' table contains information needed to use a TrueType font on a PostScript printer. It provides data for the FontInfo dictionary, the PostScript names for all glyphs, and memory usage information for the PostScript driver. PrepTable The PREP (Control Value Program) table contains instructions (bytecode) that are executed before rendering any glyph. These instructions typically perform scaling and other pre-processing needed for proper hinting.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.TtfEncoderFactory.ht.summary: Class TtfEncoderFactory Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll A factory for selecting the appropriate text encoding for TrueType name table entries. The encoding is determined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId). public static class TtfEncoderFactory Inheritance object TtfEncoderFactory Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods GetEncoding(TtfPlatFormId, TtfPlatformSpecificID, TtfLanguageID) Returns the appropriate Encoding based on the specified platform ID, platform-specific ID, and language ID. public static Encoding GetEncoding(TtfPlatFormId platformID, TtfPlatformSpecificID platformSpecificID, TtfLanguageID languageID) Parameters platformID TtfPlatFormId The TrueType platform ID. platformSpecificID TtfPlatformSpecificID The TrueType platform-specific ID. languageID TtfLanguageID The TrueType language ID. Returns Encoding The selected Encoding. For Macintosh, this returns MacRoman (code page 10000) if available; otherwise, it falls back to ASCII. For other platforms, Big‑Endian Unicode is used.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Classes HeadTable The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts. LocaRecord Represents a record from the 'loca' table of a TrueType font. NameTable The name table (tag: 'name') allows inclusion of human‑readable names for features and settings, copyright notices, font names, style names, and other information related to the font. These name strings can be provided in any language, and are referenced by other TrueType tables. NameTable.NameRecord Represents a record in the name table, defined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId) and a NameId. TTFPoint Represents a point in the TrueType glyph space. TTFTableAttribute Indicates the TrueType table associated with a class and specifies its dependencies. TableTypes Provides constants for known OpenType table tags. Includes well-known system tables such as "cmap", "glyf", "name", etc. TrueTypeFont Represents a TrueType font. TrueTypeFont.TableDeclaration Represents a declaration of a table in the TrueType font. TrueTypeGlyph Represents a TrueType glyph and implements the IGlyph interface. This class acts as a wrapper around a GlyphBase instance, delegating rendering and metric calculations. TrueTypeTable Represents the base class for a TrueType table. TtfEncoderFactory A factory for selecting the appropriate text encoding for TrueType name table entries. The encoding is determined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId). Structs Tag Represents a 4-byte identifier used in font file formats such as TrueType (TTF). Encodes a string of up to 4 ASCII characters into a 32-bit integer. Provides implicit conversions and equality comparisons with strings, integers, and byte arrays. Enums TableTypes.Tags Tags for OpenType font tables, using their 4-byte integer encodings.

api/Utils.Fonts.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts Classes FontSupport Provides utilities and encoding tables for working with font character sets and glyph name mappings. Includes standard encodings (WinAnsi, MacRoman, ISO Latin1, etc.) and predefined glyph names used in Type 1 and Type 1C fonts. Paths<T> Collects drawing paths generated while converting glyph outlines. Interfaces IFont Represents a generic font abstraction capable of retrieving glyphs and applying spacing corrections. IGlyph Represents a glyph in a font, with dimensions and rendering capabilities. IGraphicConverter Defines a graphics drawing interface for rendering glyph vector outlines. Enums CompoundGlyfFlags Flags for compound glyph components. FontBaseLine Indicates baseline positioning for typesetting in different writing systems. FontDirectionHintEnum Indicates glyph layout directionality hints. FontFlags Flags indicating various font characteristics. FontStyle Indicates stylistic attributes of a font. Le Flags permet de combiner Bold et Italic pour obtenir, par exemple, du BoldItalic. FontType Identifies the underlying technology or format of a font resource. HeadFlags Flags used in the 'head' table of a TrueType font. MacStyleFlags macStyle field flags from the 'head' table. OutlineFlags Outline point flags used in glyph definitions. TtfLanguageID Lists the language identifiers used by TrueType name records for localization. TtfNameID Represents the name identifiers stored in a TrueType font name table. TtfPlatFormId Enumerates the platform families that can appear in TrueType name records. TtfPlatformSpecificID Identifies the platform-specific encodings associated with a TtfPlatFormId.

Returns GeoPoint<T>[] An array of 2 intersection points, or an empty array if the circles are identical. Recenter(GeoVector<T>) Returns a new GeoVector such that: The current vector ("this") is mapped to (0°, 0°, 0°). The new latitude = spherical distance (central angle) from "this" to "other". The new longitude = difference in initial bearings, so that "this.Bearing" becomes 0°. The new bearing = difference in heading from "this.Bearing". Change the reference so that the current vector is effectively the new origin with heading=0. public GeoVector<T> Recenter(GeoVector<T> other) Parameters other GeoVector<T> Returns GeoVector<T> The point recentered with the current vector as new reference ToString(string, IFormatProvider) Returns a string representation of the vector using the specified format and provider. public override string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider) Parameters format string Format string applied to latitude, longitude, and bearing. formatProvider IFormatProvider Culture-specific format provider. Returns GeoVector<T> A new GeoVector<T> representing the position and bearing after traveling. api/Utils.Geography.Model.html.title: Namespace Utils.Geography.Model | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Geography.Model.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Geography.Model Classes BoundingBox<T> A BoundingBox<T> represents an immutable set of two latitude and two longitude coordinates. GeoPointList2<T> A list of GeoPointList, where each inner list represents a collection of geographic points. GeoPointList<T> A list of geographic points (GeoPoint) with additional functionality, such as calculating a bounding box. GeoPoint<T> Represents an immutable geographic point with latitude and longitude. This class supports parsing, formatting, and mathematical operations between geographic points. GeoVector<T> Represents a vector of displacement on a spherical geodesic with a bearing (heading direction). MapPosition<T> Represents an immutable map position defined by geographical coordinates and a zoom level. Planet<T> Represents a planet with geodesic properties, such as equatorial radius and circumference. Planets<T> Contains predefined planet models with their respective equatorial radii. ProjectedPoint<T> Represents an immutable point in a projected coordinate system with an associated projection transformation. Enums CoordinateDirection Enum to represent the type of geographic coordinate direction: Latitude or Longitude.

api/Utils.Geography.Projections.GallsPet.title: Class GallsPetersProjection<T> | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Geography.Projections.GallsPet.summary: Class GallsPetersProjection<T> Namespace Utils.Geography.Projections Assembly Utils.Geography.dll Implements the Gall–Peters cylindrical equal-area projection "centered" at (lat=0°, lon=0°) => (x=0, y=0). Formulas (in degrees): x = cos(45°) * longitude y = sin(latitude) / cos(45°) lon = x / cos(45°) lat = asin( y * cos(45°) ) Both input (GeoPoint) and output (ProjectedPoint) use degrees. public class GallsPetersProjection<T> : IProjectionTransformation<T> where T : struct, IFloatingPointIeee754<T> Type Parameters T A numeric type implementing IFloatingPointIeee754 (e.g. float, double, decimal). api/Utils.Geography.Projections.Mollweid.title: Class MollweideProjection<T> | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Geography.Projections.Mollweid.summary: Class MollweideProjection<T> Namespace Utils.Geography.Projections Assembly Utils.Geography.dll Mollweide (Homalographic) equal-area projection, implemented with degree-based trigonometry. Lat/Lon in degrees => (x,y) in "Mollweide units" with: (0°,0°) maps to (0,0). The bounding ellipse is roughly x ∈ [-2√2..+2√2], y ∈ [-√2..+√2]. Usage: var projector = new MollweideProjectionDegrees<double>(); var projected = projector.GeoPointToMapPoint(new GeoPoint<double>(latDeg, lonDeg)); var unprojected = projector.MapPointToGeoPoint(projected); public class MollweideProjection<T> : IProjectionTransformation<T> where T : struct, IFloatingPointIeee754<T> Type Parameters T A floating point type implementing IFloatingPointIeee754, e.g. float/double/decimal. Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Methods GeoPointToMapPoint(GeoPoint<T>) Projects (latitude, longitude) in degrees => Mollweide (x, y). Forward formula in "degree-based" Mollweide: (1) Solve 2θ + sin(2θ) = 180 * sin(lat), for θ ∈ [-90..+90]. (2) x = (2√2 / 180) * (lon) * cos(θ) (3) y = √2 * sin(θ) public ProjectedPoint<T> GeoPointToMapPoint(GeoPoint<T> geoPoint) Parameters geoPoint GeoPoint<T> Returns ProjectedPoint<T> MapPointToGeoPoint(ProjectedPoint<T>) Unprojects (x,y) in Mollweide => (lat, lon) in degrees. Inverse: (1) θ = asin( y / √2 ) [in degrees] (2) lon = x * (180 / 2√2 cos(θ)) (3) lat = asin( [2θ + sin(2θ)] / 180 ) public GeoPoint<T> MapPointToGeoPoint(ProjectedPoint<T> mapPoint) Parameters mapPoint ProjectedPoint<T> Returns GeoPoint<T>

api/Utils.Geography.Projections.html.title: Namespace Utils.Geography.Projections | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Geography.Projections.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Geography.Projections Classes EquirectangularProjection<T> A simple Equirectangular (Plate Carrée) projection that uses degrees throughout: X = longitude (in degrees) Y = latitude (in degrees) Hence (0°, 0°) => (0,0) with no offsets or scalings. If latitude exceeds ±90, it is optionally clamped. GallsPetersProjection<T> Implements the Gall–Peters cylindrical equal-area projection "centered" at (lat=0°, lon=0°) => (x=0, y=0). Formulas (in degrees): x = cos(45°) * longitude y = sin(latitude) / cos(45°) lon = x / cos(45°) lat = asin( y * cos(45°) ) Both input (GeoPoint) and output (ProjectedPoint) use degrees. LambertAzimuthalEqualArea<T> Implements the Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection for spherical coordinates. MercatorProjection<T> centered Mercator projection where (lat=0°, lon=0°) maps to (X=0, Y=0), Forward transform: X = lon Y = ln( tan(45° + lat/2) ) Inverse transform: lon = X lat = 2 * [ atan( exp(Y) ) - 45° ] MillerProjection<T> Implements the Miller cylindrical map projection. MollweideProjection<T> Mollweide (Homalographic) equal-area projection, implemented with degree-based trigonometry. Lat/Lon in degrees => (x,y) in "Mollweide units" with: (0°,0°) maps to (0,0). The bounding ellipse is roughly x ∈ [-2√2..+2√2], y ∈ [-√2..+√2]. Usage: var projector = new MollweideProjectionDegrees<double>(); var projected = projector.GeoPointToMapPoint(new GeoPoint<double>(latDeg, lonDeg)); var unprojected = projector.MapPointToGeoPoint(projected); ProjectionTransformation<T> Represents an abstract base class for map projection transformations that convert between geographic (GeoPoint<T>) and projected (ProjectedPoint<T>) coordinates. Projections<T> Provides cached access to a collection of well-known map projections. StereographicProjection<T> Implements the stereographic map projection for spherical coordinates. Interfaces IProjectionTransformation<T> Represents a generic transformation interface that converts GeoPoint<T> objects to ProjectedPoint<T> objects and vice versa.

api/Utils.IO.PartialStream.html.title: Class PartialStream | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.IO.PartialStream.html.summary: Class PartialStream Namespace Utils.IO Assembly Utils.IO.dll Provides a view onto a restricted portion (a "slice") of an underlying Stream. This partial stream starts at a specified offset within the base stream, and has a maximum length. Reading or writing beyond that partial length is not permitted. The Position property reflects the offset within the partial stream, not the offset in the underlying stream. All read and write operations are bounded by the specified length. The underlying stream's position is restored after each operation, so external consumers of the base stream see no position change (though locking is used to ensure thread safety in concurrent scenarios). By default, disposing this PartialStream does not close or dispose the underlying stream. public class PartialStream : Stream, IAsyncDisposable, IDisposable Inheritance object MarshalByRefObject Setting this value does not change the length of the underlying stream. public override long Length { get; } Property Value long Position Gets or sets the position within this partial stream. If set beyond the bounds of the partial stream, it will be clamped to [0..Length]. public override long Position { get; set; } Property Value long Methods Dispose(bool) Disposes the partial stream. By default, this does not close or dispose the underlying stream. If you need to dispose the base stream, do so externally. protected override void Dispose(bool disposing) Parameters disposing bool True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources. Flush() Clears any buffers for this stream and causes any buffered data to be written to the underlying device. public override void Flush() Read(byte[], int, int) Reads a sequence of bytes from this partial stream and advances the position within this partial stream by the number of bytes read. public override int Read(byte[] buffer, int offset, int count) Parameters buffer byte[] The buffer to store the read bytes. offset int The byte offset in buffer at which to begin storing data. count int The maximum number of bytes to read. Returns int The total number of bytes read into buffer. This might be less than the number of bytes requested if that many bytes are not currently available, or if the read is constrained by the partial stream length. Seek(long, SeekOrigin) Sets the position within this partial stream. public override long Seek(long offset, SeekOrigin origin) Parameters offset long A byte offset relative to the origin parameter. origin SeekOrigin A value of type SeekOrigin indicating the reference point. Returns long The new position within this partial stream. SetLength(long) Sets the length of the partial stream. This does not affect the underlying stream length, but changes the maximum accessible range of this partial view. public override void SetLength(long value) Parameters value long The desired length of this partial stream. Write(byte[], int, int) Writes a sequence of bytes to this partial stream and advances the position within this partial stream by the number of bytes written. public override void Write(byte[] buffer, int offset, int count) Parameters buffer byte[] The buffer containing the data to write. offset int The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin copying data. count int The number of bytes to write. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if writing the specified number of bytes would exceed the Length of the partial stream.

api/Utils.IO.StreamCopier.html.title: Class StreamCopier | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.IO.StreamCopier.html.summary: Class StreamCopier Namespace Utils.IO Assembly Utils.IO.dll A writable-only stream that copies written data to multiple target streams simultaneously. This class implements both Stream and IList<T> so that the set of target streams can be dynamically modified at runtime. Note that no concurrency control is provided; if multiple threads write concurrently, external locking may be needed. Reading and seeking are not supported. Calling Write(byte[], int, int) will broadcast the provided data to all underlying streams in the targets collection. By default, disposing this object does not dispose any of the contained streams. If the parameter closeAllTargetsOnDispose is true when constructing this class, all target streams will be disposed when Dispose() is called. public class StreamCopier : Stream, IAsyncDisposable, ReadBlock(Stream, byte[]) Reads and returns the next "block" from the stream, delimited by the specified byte sequence. The returned array does not include the delimiter itself. If the delimiter is not found before EOF, all remaining bytes in the stream are returned. This method can be called repeatedly to read multiple blocks from the same stream. public static byte[] ReadBlock(this Stream s, byte[] blockSeparator) Parameters s Stream The Stream from which to read the block. blockSeparator byte[] A byte array representing the block delimiter. ReadBytes(Stream, int, bool) Reads exactly length bytes from the given Stream and returns them in a byte array. public static byte[] ReadBytes(this Stream s, int length, bool raiseException = false) Parameters s Stream The source Stream to read from. length int The number of bytes to read from the stream. raiseException bool If true and fewer than length bytes could be read, throws an EndOfStreamException. Returns byte[] A byte array containing up to length bytes read from the stream. If raiseException is false and the stream ends, the returned array might be partially uninitialized for the remainder. Exceptions EndOfStreamException Thrown if raiseException is true and the stream ends before length bytes could be read. api/Utils.IO.StreamValidator.html.summary: Class StreamValidator Namespace Utils.IO Assembly Utils.IO.dll A write-only buffering stream that defers writing to a target stream until Validate() is called. Data written to this stream is held in an internal buffer. When Validate() is invoked, the buffered data is flushed into the underlying target stream. If Discard() is called, the buffered data is discarded without being written to the target. This class does not support reading or seeking. After you finish all writes, you can either commit them by calling Validate() or discard them via Discard(). The underlying stream is not closed or disposed automatically when this stream is disposed (override Dispose(bool) if you need that behavior). public class StreamValidator : Stream, IAsyncDisposable, api/Utils.IO.html.summary: Namespace Utils.IO Classes PartialStream Provides a view onto a restricted portion (a "slice") of an underlying Stream. This partial stream starts at a specified offset within the base stream, and has a maximum length. Reading or writing beyond that partial length is not permitted. The Position property reflects the offset within the partial stream, not the offset in the underlying stream. All read and write operations are bounded by the specified length. The underlying stream's position is restored after each operation, so external consumers of the base stream see no position change (though locking is used to ensure thread safety in concurrent scenarios). By default, disposing this PartialStream does not close or dispose the underlying stream. StreamCopier A writable-only stream that copies written data to multiple target streams simultaneously. This class implements both Stream and IList<T> so that the set of target streams can be dynamically modified at runtime. Note that no concurrency control is provided; if multiple threads write concurrently, external locking may be needed. Reading and seeking are not supported. Calling Write(byte[], int, int) will broadcast the provided data to all underlying streams in the targets collection. By default, disposing this object does not dispose any of the contained streams. If the parameter closeAllTargetsOnDispose is true when constructing this class, all target streams will be disposed when Dispose() is called. StreamUtils Provides extension and utility methods for working with Stream objects. StreamValidator A write-only buffering stream that defers writing to a target stream until Validate() is called. Data written to this stream is held in an internal buffer. When Validate() is invoked, the buffered data is flushed into the underlying target stream. If Discard() is called, the buffered data is discarded without being written to the target. This class does not support reading or seeking. After you finish all writes, you can either commit them by calling Validate() or discard them via Discard(). The underlying stream is not closed or disposed automatically when this stream is disposed (override Dispose(bool) if you need that behavior).

api/Utils.Imaging.html.title: Namespace Utils.Imaging | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Imaging.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Imaging Classes BitmapAccessor Provides direct indexed access to a bitmap. BitmapArgb32Accessor Provides direct memory access to 32-bit ARGB bitmap data, enabling fast pixel manipulation scenarios. BitmapArgb64Accessor Provides direct memory access to 64-bit ARGB bitmap data for high precision pixel manipulation scenarios. BitmapIndexed8Accessor Provides direct memory access to 8-bit indexed bitmap data. ColorAhsv HSV color representation using double components. ColorAhsv32 HSV color stored with 8-bit components. ColorAhsv64 HSV color representation using 16-bit components. ColorBlend Provides color blending operations similar to those found in image editing software. ConvolutionMatrixFactory Provides common convolution kernels for image processing. ImageAccessorExtensions Provides helper methods for IImageAccessor<T> instances. ImageUtils Provides helpers to resolve image encoders and decoders by MIME type. MatrixImageTransformer<A, T> Applies a weighted matrix transformation to an image. Structs ColorArgb Represents an ARGB color using double components. ColorArgb32 Represents a 32-bit ARGB color using byte components. ColorArgb64 Represents a 64-bit ARGB color using 16-bit components. Interfaces IColorAhsv<T> Represents a color value expressed through alpha, hue, saturation, and value channels. IColorArgbConvertible<TSelf, TArgb, T> Defines a color system that can convert to and from an ARGB representation. IColorArgb<T> Represents a color value expressed through alpha, red, green, and blue channels. IImageAccessor<T> Describes an abstraction capable of exposing read/write access to a two-dimensional image resource. IImageAccessor<A, T> Provides strongly typed access to an image whose pixels are represented by an ARGB color structure. IImageTransformer<A, T> Defines a generic transformation that can be applied to an image.
