Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Constructors IntRange() Creates an empty set of intervals. public IntRange() IntRange(string, CultureInfo) Creates a set of intervals from a string. Expects forms like: "∞-∞" => all integers "∞-5" => [-∞..5] "5-∞" => [5..+∞] "2-5" => [2..5] "42" => single value [42..42] And possibly multiple intervals separated by commas or semicolons, e.g. "∞-0, 5-10, 42". public IntRange(string input, CultureInfo cultureInfo) Parameters input string cultureInfo CultureInfo IntRange(string, TextInfo) Creates a set of intervals from a string. Expects forms like: "∞-∞" => all integers "∞-5" => [-∞..5] "5-∞" => [5..+∞] "2-5" => [2..5] "42" => single value [42..42] And possibly multiple intervals separated by commas or semicolons, e.g. "∞-0, 5-10, 42". public IntRange(string input, TextInfo textInfo) Parameters input string textInfo TextInfo IntRange(string, params string[]) Creates a set of intervals from a string. Expects forms like: "∞-∞" => all integers "∞-5" => [-∞..5] "5-∞" => [5..+∞] "2-5" => [2..5] "42" => single value [42..42] And possibly multiple intervals separated by commas or semicolons, e.g. "∞-0, 5-10, 42". public IntRange(string input, params string[] separators) Parameters input string separators string[] Properties FullRange Returns an IntRange representing all integers from -∞ to +∞. (A single interval with null-min, null-max). public static IntRange<T> FullRange { get; } Property Value IntRange<T> Methods Complement() Computes the complement with respect to the full integer domain (-∞ to +∞). That is, all values in [-∞..+∞] that are not in this IntRange<T>. public IntRange<T> Complement() Returns IntRange<T> Contains(T) Checks whether an integer value is contained in any of the intervals. public bool Contains(T value) Parameters value T Returns bool Except(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Produces the difference of two sets: all values in left that are not in right. public static IntRange<T> Except(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> GetEnumerator() Enumerates all integers in the stored intervals. If you have intervals that go to ±∞, enumerating them is infinite! For safety, you might want to throw if the range is unbounded. public IEnumerator<T> GetEnumerator() Returns IEnumerator<T> Intersect(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Produces the intersection of two IntRange sets (&). public static IntRange<T> Intersect(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> SymmetricDifference(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Symmetric difference => (A \ B) ∪ (B \ A). public static IntRange<T> SymmetricDifference(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> ToString() Converts the current IntRange<T> to its string representation using default formatting. public override string ToString() Returns string A string that represents the current range. ToString(IFormatProvider) Converts the current IntRange<T> to its string representation using the provided format provider. public string ToString(IFormatProvider formatProvider) Parameters formatProvider IFormatProvider Culture used when formatting the numeric boundaries. Returns string A string that represents the current range. ToString(string?) Converts the current IntRange<T> to its string representation using the provided format string. public string ToString(string? format) Parameters format string Custom format applied to the numeric boundaries. Returns string A string that represents the current range. ToString(string, IFormatProvider) Joins each range with the current culture's list separator (or the formatProvider's culture). Each range is printed as "∞-∞", "∞-5", "5-∞", etc. public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider) Parameters format string formatProvider IFormatProvider Returns string Union(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Produces the union of two IntRange sets (|). public static IntRange<T> Union(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> Operators operator &(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Bitwise AND => Intersection public static IntRange<T> operator &(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> operator |(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Bitwise OR => Union public static IntRange<T> operator |(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> operator ^(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Bitwise XOR => Symmetric Difference public static IntRange<T> operator ^(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> operator ~(IntRange<T>) Bitwise NOT => Complement (everything in [-∞..+∞] not in this range). public static IntRange<T> operator ~(IntRange<T> range) Parameters range IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T> operator -(IntRange<T>, IntRange<T>) Substraction => Except public static IntRange<T> operator -(IntRange<T> left, IntRange<T> right) Parameters left IntRange<T> right IntRange<T> Returns IntRange<T>

api/Utils.Range.Ranges-1.html.summary: Class Ranges<T> Namespace Utils.Range Assembly Utils.dll Represents a set of intervals (ranges) for an ordered type T. Provides set-like operations (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference) via bitwise operators: | => Union, & => Intersection, ^ => Symmetric Difference, ~ => Complement (relative to entire domain, if desired), => Difference (Except). api/Utils.Range.html.title: Namespace Utils.Range | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Range.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Range Classes DateTimeRanges Represents a collection of ranges for DateTime values, with parsing support from string representations. DoubleRanges Represents a collection of ranges for double values, with parsing support from string representations. IntRange<T> Represents a collection of integer intervals (ranges), each of which may include negative or positive infinity as an endpoint. Bitwise-like operators for set operations: | (Union), & (Intersection), ^ (Symmetric Difference), ~ (Complement). Internally stores multiple non-overlapping intervals sorted by their (Min, Max). A null Minimum => -∞. A null Maximum => +∞. RangeUtils Provides extension methods for creating and manipulating ranges on read-only lists. Ranges<T> Represents a set of intervals (ranges) for an ordered type T. Provides set-like operations (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference) via bitwise operators: | => Union, & => Intersection, ^ => Symmetric Difference, ~ => Complement (relative to entire domain, if desired), => Difference (Except). The intervals are stored internally as disjoint, sorted Range<T> objects. ReadOnlyRange<T> Represents a read-only range of elements in a list, supporting custom stepping and indexing. SingleRanges Represents a collection of ranges for float values, with parsing support from string representations. Structs Range<T> Represents a range of values with a start and end, plus inclusivity flags. Stored as a struct for efficient copying.

api/Utils.Reflection.Platform.html.summary: Class Platform Namespace Utils.Reflection Assembly Utils.Reflection.dll Utility class for runtime platform and architecture detection. public static class Platform Inheritance object Platform Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties IsAndroid True if runtime platform is Android. public static bool IsAndroid { get; } Property Value bool IsArm True if process architecture is Arm (32-bit). public static bool IsArm { get; } Property Value bool IsArm64 True if process architecture is Arm64 (64-bit ARM). public static bool IsArm64 { get; } Property Value bool IsBrowser True if runtime platform is running on WebAssembly in a browser. public static bool IsBrowser { get; } Property Value bool IsIOS True if runtime platform is iOS. public static bool IsIOS { get; } Property Value bool IsLinux True if runtime platform is Linux (desktop/server). public static bool IsLinux { get; } Property Value bool IsMacOsX True if runtime platform is macOS. public static bool IsMacOsX { get; } Property Value bool IsS390x True if process architecture is IBM s390x. public static bool IsS390x { get; } Property Value bool IsTvOS True if runtime platform is tvOS. public static bool IsTvOS { get; } Property Value bool IsUnknown True if runtime platform is unknown or not explicitly handled above. public static bool IsUnknown { get; } Property Value bool IsUnknownArchitecture True if process architecture is unknown or not explicitly handled above. public static bool IsUnknownArchitecture { get; } Property Value bool IsWasm True if process architecture is WebAssembly. public static bool IsWasm { get; } Property Value bool IsWatchOS True if runtime platform is watchOS. public static bool IsWatchOS { get; } Property Value bool IsWindows True if runtime platform is Windows. public static bool IsWindows { get; } Property Value bool IsX64 True if process architecture is x64 (64-bit Intel/AMD). public static bool IsX64 { get; } Property Value bool IsX86 True if process architecture is x86 (32-bit Intel/AMD). public static bool IsX86 { get; } Property Value bool NativeULongSize Size of native (unmanaged) 'ulong' (e.g., CK_ULONG in PKCS#11). Default logic: On Linux/macOS (including iOS, Android) it depends on process bitness (4 bytes on 32-bit, 8 bytes on 64-bit). On Windows it is 4 bytes, regardless of x86/x64. This value can be overridden by setter if needed. public static int NativeULongSize { get; } Property Value int StructPackingSize Controls alignment of unmanaged struct fields: On Windows, CRYPTOKI structs are usually packed with 1-byte alignment. On Unix-like platforms, they are usually packed with default alignment (0). This can be overridden by setter if needed. public static int StructPackingSize { get; set; } Property Value int Uses32BitRuntime True if 32-bit runtime is used (x86, Arm32, etc.). public static bool Uses32BitRuntime { get; } Property Value bool Uses64BitRuntime True if 64-bit runtime is used (x64, Arm64, s390x, or other 64-bit). public static bool Uses64BitRuntime { get; } Property Value bool

api/Utils.Reflection.Reflection.Emit.Emi.summary: Class EmitDllMappableClass Namespace Utils.Reflection.Reflection.Emit Assembly Utils.Reflection.dll Utility class for dynamically generating types that map to DLL functions. api/Utils.Reflection.html.title: Namespace Utils.Reflection | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Reflection.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Reflection Classes DelegateInvoker<TBaseArg, TResult> A utility class that allows dynamic invocation of delegate methods based on the runtime type of an argument. ExtendedInvoker<TResult> An invoker that can store multiple delegates sharing the same return type but with different parameter lists. On invocation, the best matching delegate is selected using the compiler's distance-based method search. ExternalAttribute Attribute used to mark properties or fields to be mapped to external unmanaged functions. LibraryMapper A class to dynamically map unmanaged DLL functions to .NET properties or fields. This class also handles platform differences (Windows vs. Unix-based systems). MultiDelegateInvoker<TBaseArg, TResult> Invokes multiple delegates registered for a given argument type. Platform Utility class for runtime platform and architecture detection. PropertyOrFieldInfo Represents a reflection wrapper around either a PropertyInfo or a FieldInfo, providing unified access to both for getting and setting values, as well as attribute retrieval. ReflectionEx Provides extension methods to simplify reflection-based operations. TypeEx Provides extension methods for various Type reflection operations.

api/Utils.String.html.summary: Namespace Utils.String Classes Brackets Represents a pair of characters that mark the beginning and end of a delimited block. CharsComparer Specialized comparer for sequences of char (e.g., strings, char arrays, lists). Ensures efficient comparisons between strings, char arrays, spans, and other character sequences, with support for culture-specific or case-insensitive comparisons. CharsEqualityComparer Specialized comparer for sequences of char (e.g., strings, char arrays, lists). Ensures efficient equality checks between strings, char arrays, spans, and other character sequences, with support for culture-specific or case-insensitive comparisons. StringChange Represents a change between two strings. StringConverter Provides functionality to convert strings into various types using custom and predefined converters. StringDifference Compares two strings and returns their differences as a list of changes. StringExtensions Provides extension methods for string manipulation, trimming, prefix/suffix removal, wildcard matching, default-value substitutions, etc. StringUtils Provides helper methods for working with strings, including trimming brackets and parsing delimited content. Enums StringComparisonStatus Indicates the status of a comparison between two strings (Added, Removed, Unchanged).

api/Utils.VirtualMachine.html.title: Namespace Utils.VirtualMachine | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.VirtualMachine.html.summary: Namespace Utils.VirtualMachine Classes Context Represents the base execution context used by VirtualProcessor<T>. DefaultContext A default context implementation providing an object stack. InstructionAttribute Identifies a method as a virtual machine instruction and stores its metadata. NumberReader Factory methods for obtaining INumberReader instances. VirtualProcessorException Represents errors that occur while executing a VirtualProcessor<T>. VirtualProcessor<T> Provides an abstract base class for a virtual processor that interprets and executes instructions from a byte array-based context. Instructions are defined by methods annotated with InstructionAttribute. Interfaces INumberReader Provides methods to read numbers from a virtual machine Context. Delegates VirtualProcessor<T>.InstructionDelegate Represents a delegate to handle an instruction, given the current context.

Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Remarks Derive from this class to define XML-processing logic. Annotate your handler methods with MatchAttribute to match specific XPath expressions, and optionally use XmlNamespaceAttribute at the class level to register namespace prefixes. Constructors XmlDataProcessor() Initializes the XmlDataProcessor, sets up namespace management, and prepares method triggers based on MatchAttribute usage. protected XmlDataProcessor() Properties Current The current XML node being processed. This is updated internally while methods are being invoked. protected XPathNavigator Current { get; } Property Value XPathNavigator NamespaceManager Manages XML namespaces for the XPath expressions. public XmlNamespaceManager NamespaceManager { get; } Property Value XmlNamespaceManager Remarks Populate this using XmlNamespaceAttribute or manually by adding calls to AddNamespace(string, string). Methods Apply(string, params object[]) Evaluates the specified xPath string from the current node and invokes any matching handler methods on each resulting node. protected void Apply(string xPath, params object[] parameters) Parameters xPath string The XPath string to evaluate. parameters object[] Parameters passed to the invoked methods. Apply(XPathExpression, params object[]) Evaluates the specified compiled xPath from the current node and invokes any matching handler methods on each resulting node. protected void Apply(XPathExpression xPath, params object[] parameters) Parameters xPath XPathExpression The pre-compiled XPath expression. parameters object[] Parameters passed to the invoked methods. Apply(XPathNavigator, params object[]) Invokes any matching handler method(s) on the single node. protected void Apply(XPathNavigator node, params object[] parameters) Parameters node XPathNavigator The XML node to process. parameters object[] Parameters passed to the invoked methods. Apply(XPathNavigator, string, params object[]) Evaluates the specified xPath string from the given node and invokes any matching handler methods on each resulting node. protected void Apply(XPathNavigator node, string xPath, params object[] parameters) Parameters node XPathNavigator The starting node. xPath string XPath string to evaluate. parameters object[] Parameters passed to the invoked methods. Apply(XPathNavigator, XPathExpression, params object[]) Evaluates the specified pre-compiled xPath from the given node and invokes any matching handler methods on each resulting node. protected void Apply(XPathNavigator node, XPathExpression xPath, params object[] parameters) Parameters node XPathNavigator The starting node. xPath XPathExpression A pre-compiled XPath expression. parameters object[] Parameters passed to the invoked methods. CreateExpression(string) Creates and compiles an XPathExpression using the current XmlNamespaceManager. protected XPathExpression CreateExpression(string xPath) Parameters xPath string The XPath string to be compiled. Returns XPathExpression A compiled XPathExpression. GetNodes(string) Retrieves nodes matching the specified xPath from the current node. protected XPathNodeIterator GetNodes(string xPath) Parameters xPath string The XPath string to evaluate. Returns XPathNodeIterator An XPathNodeIterator over the matching nodes. GetNodes(XPathExpression) Retrieves nodes matching the specified pre-compiled xPath from the current node. protected XPathNodeIterator GetNodes(XPathExpression xPath) Parameters xPath XPathExpression The compiled XPath expression. Returns XPathNodeIterator An XPathNodeIterator over the matching nodes. Read(Stream) Reads and processes an XML document from the specified Stream. public void Read(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream The Stream containing the XML document. Read(string) Reads and processes an XML document from the specified URI path. public void Read(string uri) Parameters uri string The URI to the XML document. Read(XPathNavigator) Reads and processes an XML document from a given XPathNavigator. Invokes the Root() method (or any other triggers matching "/") on the root node. public void Read(XPathNavigator navigator) Parameters navigator XPathNavigator The XPathNavigator to process. Read(XmlReader) Reads and processes an XML document from the specified XmlReader. public void Read(XmlReader reader) Parameters reader XmlReader The XmlReader positioned at the start of the XML document. Root() Abstract method that gets invoked for the XML root ("/"). Derived classes must implement this to handle root-level logic. [Match("/")] protected abstract void Root() ValueOf(string) Returns the value of the current node, or the node matched by xPath (if specified). protected string ValueOf(string xPath = ".") Parameters xPath string An optional XPath string to evaluate (defaults to "."). Returns string The textual value of the matching node, or null if no match is found.

api/Utils.XML.html.summary: Namespace Utils.XML Classes MatchAttribute Attribute for associating a method with a specific XPath expression. When the driver encounters a node matching XPathExpression, it invokes the method tagged with this attribute. XmlDataProcessor Abstract base class for XML-driven functionality. The class automatically calls methods based on MatchAttribute triggers (XPath expressions). XmlNamespaceAttribute Attribute to define XML namespace mappings for XPath expressions. Use multiple attributes if you need multiple prefix-to-namespace bindings. XmlUtils Utility class for XML-related helper methods.

index.html.title: All-purpose Utilities | omy.Utils Documentation
index.html.summary: All-purpose Utilities All-purpose-Utilities is a family of focused .NET libraries published on NuGet under the omy.Utils prefix. It is designed for consumers who want small, task-oriented packages (networking, I/O, data mapping, parser tooling, source generators, and more) without adopting a single monolithic framework. Quick links Getting started — installation and first steps API Reference — full generated API documentation Parser documentation — ANTLR4-compatible parser runtime Changelog NuGet packages
